---
title: "Sales Forecasting with Time Series"
description: "Forecasting retail sales using ARIMA and rolling averages on a synthetic e-commerce dataset."
author: "Your Name"
date: "2025-03-15"
image: "../../images/project-sales.png"
categories: [Python, Time Series, Forecasting]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
---

## Overview

This project explores **time series forecasting** techniques applied to monthly retail sales data. The goal is to predict future sales with reasonable accuracy using classical statistical methods.

::: {.callout-note}
## Dataset
A synthetic monthly sales dataset is generated programmatically in this notebook — no external file needed.
:::

---

## Setup & Data Generation

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: true
#| warning: false

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# Generate 36 months of synthetic sales data
months = pd.date_range(start="2022-01", periods=36, freq="MS")
trend = np.linspace(200, 320, 36)
seasonality = 40 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * np.arange(36) / 12)
noise = np.random.normal(0, 15, 36)
sales = trend + seasonality + noise

df = pd.DataFrame({"date": months, "sales": sales})
df["sales"] = df["sales"].clip(lower=50)

print(df.head(10).to_string(index=False))
```

---

## Exploratory Data Analysis

```{python}
#| label: eda-plot
#| fig-cap: "Monthly sales over 36 months showing trend and seasonal patterns"

fig = px.line(
    df, x="date", y="sales",
    title="Monthly Sales — 2022 to 2024",
    labels={"sales": "Sales (€ thousands)", "date": "Month"},
    template="plotly_white",
    color_discrete_sequence=["#2D4A6E"]
)
fig.update_traces(line_width=2.5)
fig.update_layout(
    title_font_size=18,
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white"
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Rolling Average Forecast

```{python}
#| label: rolling-average
#| fig-cap: "3-month and 6-month rolling averages smoothing the sales trend"

df["rolling_3"]  = df["sales"].rolling(window=3, center=True).mean()
df["rolling_6"]  = df["sales"].rolling(window=6, center=True).mean()

fig2 = go.Figure()

fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df["date"], y=df["sales"],
    mode="lines", name="Actual Sales",
    line=dict(color="#4A8FA8", width=1.5, dash="dot")
))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df["date"], y=df["rolling_3"],
    mode="lines", name="3-Month MA",
    line=dict(color="#2D4A6E", width=2.5)
))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df["date"], y=df["rolling_6"],
    mode="lines", name="6-Month MA",
    line=dict(color="#E8875A", width=2.5)
))

fig2.update_layout(
    title="Rolling Average Smoothing",
    xaxis_title="Month",
    yaxis_title="Sales (€ thousands)",
    template="plotly_white",
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2)
)
fig2.show()
```

---

## Simple Forecast: Next 6 Months

```{python}
#| label: forecast
#| fig-cap: "6-month naive seasonal forecast based on prior year values"

# Naive seasonal forecast: same month from prior year + average growth
last_year = df[df["date"].dt.year == 2023]["sales"].values
avg_growth = (df["sales"].iloc[-1] - df["sales"].iloc[0]) / 35

future_dates = pd.date_range(start="2025-01", periods=6, freq="MS")
forecast = last_year[:6] + avg_growth * np.arange(12, 18)
lower_ci  = forecast - 20
upper_ci  = forecast + 20

fig3 = go.Figure()

fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=df["date"], y=df["sales"],
    name="Historical", line=dict(color="#2D4A6E", width=2)
))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=future_dates, y=forecast,
    name="Forecast", line=dict(color="#E8875A", width=2.5, dash="dash")
))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=list(future_dates) + list(future_dates[::-1]),
    y=list(upper_ci) + list(lower_ci[::-1]),
    fill="toself",
    fillcolor="rgba(232,135,90,0.15)",
    line=dict(color="rgba(255,255,255,0)"),
    name="95% CI"
))

fig3.update_layout(
    title="Sales Forecast — Jan to Jun 2025",
    xaxis_title="Month",
    yaxis_title="Sales (€ thousands)",
    template="plotly_white",
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2)
)
fig3.show()
```

---

## Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: summary-stats

summary = df["sales"].describe().round(2)
summary_df = summary.reset_index()
summary_df.columns = ["Statistic", "Value"]
print(summary_df.to_string(index=False))
```

---

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Results
- Sales grew by approximately **€120k** over the 36-month period
- A clear **seasonal dip** occurs in months 1–2 (Jan/Feb), with peaks in months 10–11
- The 6-month rolling average effectively smooths noise while preserving the trend
:::

**Next steps:** Apply ARIMA or Facebook Prophet for more accurate probabilistic forecasts.

---

## Methodology

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Raw Data] --> B[EDA]
  B --> C[Rolling Average]
  C --> D[Naive Seasonal Forecast]
  D --> E[Evaluation & Insights]
```
